references.text: [Er37] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, Note on the number of prime divisors of integers. J. London Math. Soc. (1937), 308-314.

remarks.text: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er37} proved that the density of integers $n$ with $\omega(n)>\log\log n$ is $1/2$. The Chinese remainder theorem implies that there is such an interval with\[\lvert I\rvert \geq (1+o(1))\frac{\log x}{(\log\log x)^2}.\]It could be true that there is such an interval of length $(\log x)^{k}$ for arbitrarily large $k$.

statement.text: Let $\omega(n)$ count the number of distinct prime factors of $n$. What is the size of the largest interval $I\subseteq [x,2x]$ such that $\omega(n)>\log\log n$ for all $n\in I$?
